title: Xtra Garlicky Tunisian Chicken Pilaf
description: A flavorful and aromatic Tunisian dish featuring chicken, garlic, and rice, perfect for a family dinner. This recipe is kid-friendly and incorporates ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the saucepan and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the saucepan and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the rice wine to the saucepan and cook until the liquid is almost completely evaporated, about 2 minutes.
Add the uncooked white rice to the saucepan and stir to combine with the chicken and garlic mixture.
Add the chicken broth, salt, black pepper, and dried thyme to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer for 20 minutes, until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
